title: ipvlan does not see dhcp.sock
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

As a workaround of the other issue - now I wanted to try out ipvlan (L3/L3s), since I could use firewall rules and masquerading.
I create the network "public" for that.
reverse-proxy container added to that - but the container doesn't start because dhcp.sock canot be found.

looked where dhcp is -> /opt/cni/bin/dhcp
started dhcp with root and later as a normal user - no success

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman network create -d ipvlan -o mode=l3 --ipv6 public`

2. `podman create --name=reverse-proxy --net public -p 8080:80 -p 8443:443 docker.io/library/nginx:1-alpine`

3. make sure `/run/cni` is writable by the same user
4. `/opt/cni/bin/dhcp daemon`
5. open a second terminal/session:\
`podman start reverse-proxy`
6. error appears immediately

**Describe the results you received:**
```
WARN[0000] Failed to load cached network config: network public not found in CNI cache, falling back to loading network public from disk 
WARN[0000] 1 error occurred:
	* plugin type="ipvlan" failed (delete): cni plugin ipvlan failed: error dialing DHCP daemon: dial unix /run/cni/dhcp.sock: connect: no such file or directory
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
container should be started and should get an IP from dhcp(?)
That's my theory - I wanted to experiment first.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
```shell
$ ls -l /run/cni/dhcp.sock
srwxr-x--- 1 pods pods 0 Feb  3 23:05 /run/cni/dhcp.sock
```
**Output of `podman version`:**

```

```

comments.body:
You cannot really use ipvlan/macvlan as rootless user. To make the rootless networking work we have to use an extra network and mount namespace. The network namespace will be connected via slirp4netns to the host. see `podman unshare --rootless-netns` to join this ns.
To make cni work we have to bind mount new cni directories to into this namespace so that we can write to it. That is why it cannot find the dhcp socket. But even if it were there you could still not use it since it requires root permissions to use it. Also the ipvlan/macvlan plugin would be unable to find your host device in this namespace.
The rootless container ip addresses are not routeable anyway so it does not make sense to use dhcp. Eveything has to be proxied trough slirp4netns.

Then the only workaround would be:
- create a host-reverse proxy (without podman) with proxy-headers
- forward all traffic to the published ports of the reverse-proxy container (with multiple networks)
- use the proxy-headers to regain the original IP

While this will "somehow" work with http/https traffic, this won't work for the mailserver. There I cannot even use fail2ban because of podmans limitations with multiple networks and port_handler=slirp4netns.

No other workaround comes in my mind...
The mailserver container must not have multiple networks - then I have to publish the DB-port also, to be able to let them connect.


Feels like every workaround I try - is a dead end.
